anti_claims: This organ JSON seed does not flip source authority away from core/organ_atlas.json and core/organ_registry.json.
Required paper/mechanism/code links prove declared structural references only, not runtime correctness or release readiness.
Selective concept, principle, axiom, and sibling-organ edges are not inferred from prose, specialty, family, or evidence class.
Absent selective organ edges are residual pressure, not evidence that no neighbours exist.
Generated markdown, graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

code_loci.role: Runtime authority for bounded verifier-transition fixture validation, exported execution-bundle validation, source-module manifest checks, Lean/Lake tool-witness receipt writing, transition safety checks, and negative-case enforcement.

mechanism_refs.edge_justification: The organ's source module, first-wave fixture, verifier execution receipts, source-module manifest, transition safety counters, authority ceiling, and negative cases validate bounded public Lean transition witnesses without counting oracle/provider output as proof, exporting proof bodies, mutating source, claiming benchmark solve rates, or authorizing release.

omitted: runtime outputs beyond named public receipts
selective doctrine neighbours not named by atlas source rows
omission_receipt.reason: Organ instance preserves public-safe atlas/registry fields. Required links resolve only when source ids or paths resolve; selective law/concept/principle edges remain residual unless source evidence names ids.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Populate missing paper/mechanism/code links from source evidence, then bind concept/principle/axiom/wires_to edges only when ids are named.

organ_payload.claim_ceiling: It is a tool-witness receipt for bounded public Lean transition rows only: it does not establish general proof authority, count oracle/provider output as proof, export proof bodies or tactic scripts, call providers, mutate source, claim benchmark solve-rates, or authorize release/publication.

source_atlas_row.agent_gloss: Run it for a structured, leak-proof receipt of which bounded Lean transition candidates the local Lean/Lake checker (run on a temp copy of a public fixture) accepted or rejected, with residual-retry and bounded policy reruns re-checked the same way. Proof bodies, raw tactic scripts, and provider/oracle text are excluded (proof_body_exported=False, body_in_receipt=False); the receipt carries only ids, action classes, Lean return codes, accept/residual verdicts, safety counters, authority ceiling, and anti-claim.
source_atlas_row.claim_ceiling_restated: It is a tool-witness receipt for bounded public Lean transition rows only: it does not establish general proof authority, count oracle/provider output as proof, export proof bodies or tactic scripts, call providers, mutate source, claim benchmark solve-rates, or authorize release/publication.

code_loci.role: Runtime authority for bounded verifier-transition fixture validation, exported execution-bundle validation, source-module manifest checks, Lean/Lake tool-witness receipt writing, transition safety checks, and negative-case enforcement.

source_atlas_row.human_gloss: It copies a small public Lean math project into a throwaway temporary workspace and actually runs the Lean/Lake checker on a handful of small, bounded proof-step attempts the tool builds itself. It then writes down what the checker said: which attempts were accepted, which failed, and the failure category for each, plus safety counts (for example, how many attempts tried to sneak in forbidden content and were rejected). The pass/fail facts and the safety counts are readable directly, while the tool never shows the underlying proof text, never calls any outside service, and never modifies the original project or any existing source files.

source_atlas_row.wiring_note: Self-contained Lean-execution replay specimen over its own fixture; binds only to the shared kernel + secret-exclusion/receipts/authority spine.
